author: claude
subject: review
summary: Claude CLI review of the current uncommitted follow-up pass after review 42d9e774. Finding [Resolved - prior Medium]: the flock-vs-Layer-4-read contention finding was addressed by adding FileTapeFileJournal::open_shared_for_replay / FileTapeFileJournalReader, re-exporting the reader, documenting the Layer 4 shared replay path and retry semantics, and adding tests for no-truncate replay plus shared/exclusive locking. The conservative FUA policy was also documented in code and docs. Finding [Low]: Layer 4 docs and API still allowed a reader handle to be held across SQLite work, which could block a new append session; recommended either consume the reader in load_committed or state a strict drop-before-SQLite rule. Finding [Low]: FileTapeFileJournalReader::load_committed reopened the journal by path instead of reading through the locked descriptor, leaving a rename/replace TOCTOU window; the same pattern existed in FileTapeFileJournal::load_committed. Recommended replaying through the already-locked descriptor. Claude did not run cargo verification in that session; it inspected journal.rs, lib.rs, docs/layer3c-design.md, docs/layer4-implementation-addendum-v0.2.md, docs/README.md, and the prior review entry.
verification: Claude read code/docs and git diff context only; no cargo command was run by Claude.
residual_risk: No independent cargo run by Claude.
Conservative FUA policy still lacks a future allowlist or flush-capability probe.
No real power-cut validation of the journal durability precondition.
No concurrent stress test of the shared replay retry loop.

author: chatgpt
subject: implementation: shared journal replay follow-up and QuadStor gates
summary: Completed the user-requested first four next steps. Loaded scratch cartridge RMN001L9 into QuadStor drive 0 after the first journaled live gate failed with NoMedium, then reran quadstor_parity_journaled_session successfully and ran the full ignored quadstor_parity suite successfully (6/6) against /dev/sg0, library 7CBAD9CF74, bay 0x0100. Asked Claude CLI for a fresh review of the current pass. Agreed with both Low follow-ups and fixed them: FileTapeFileJournalReader::load_committed now consumes the shared replay handle, FileTapeFileJournalReader and FileTapeFileJournal replay through try_clone() of the already-locked descriptor instead of reopening by path, JournalError exposes is_lock_contended() for Layer 4 retry logic, and the Layer 3c / Layer 4 docs now require replay handles to be dropped before SQLite work and define append-session retry behavior while ingestion is active. Unloaded RMN001L9 back to slot 1 after the live suite. This run also prepares the dirty Layer 3c v0.7.2 journal/parity_map/doc worktree for a checkpoint commit while leaving unrelated scratch files untracked.

hardware.cleanup: Unloaded drive 0 back into storage element 1.

verification: sudo -n env PATH="$PATH" HOME="$HOME" REM_QUADSTOR_PARITY_DRIVE_PATH=/dev/sg0 REM_QUADSTOR_PARITY_WRITE_LOOP=1 REM_QUADSTOR_PARITY_LIBRARY_SERIAL=7CBAD9CF74 REM_QUADSTOR_PARITY_DRIVE_BAY=0x0100 cargo test -p remanence-parity --test quadstor_parity quadstor_parity_journaled_session -- --ignored --test-threads=1 --nocapture

sudo -n env PATH="$PATH" HOME="$HOME" REM_QUADSTOR_PARITY_DRIVE_PATH=/dev/sg0 REM_QUADSTOR_PARITY_WRITE_LOOP=1 REM_QUADSTOR_PARITY_LIBRARY_SERIAL=7CBAD9CF74 REM_QUADSTOR_PARITY_DRIVE_BAY=0x0100 cargo test -p remanence-parity --test quadstor_parity -- --ignored --test-threads=1 --nocapture

claude_review_check.before: Latest Claude review 42d9e774 had one Medium on Layer 4 shared journal replay and one Low on conservative FUA policy.
claude_review_check.after: Fresh Claude CLI review 5f11bead found the prior Medium resolved, then raised two Low API/doc concerns. Both were addressed in this run.

author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry cb34bc5e-54b0-404f-87f0-6ccf79ad40ec (Layer 4 shared-replay API hardening + live QuadStor journaled gate: load_committed consumes the reader, both reader and writer paths replay through try_clone() of the locked fd, JournalError::is_lock_contended() helper, docs require drop-before-SQLite and define append/ingestion retry, full ignored QuadStor suite 6/6 against RMN001L9 in bay 0x0100). Confirmed FileTapeFileJournalReader::load_committed at crates/remanence-parity/src/journal.rs:394 takes `self` by value (line 394 signature `pub fn load_committed(self)`), so the shared flock is released when the call returns — Layer 4 cannot retain the reader across SQLite work. Both replay paths now go through `self.file.try_clone()?` (crates/remanence-parity/src/journal.rs:395 and 436) and reuse load_committed_from_reader with TruncateTornTail::No on the reader (line 404) vs TruncateTornTail::Yes on the writer (line 445), so read-only replay never mutates the file and live append-session replay still self-heals torn trailing records — closes the rename/replace TOCTOU window the prior CLI review (uuid 5f11bead) flagged. JournalError::is_lock_contended (crates/remanence-parity/src/journal.rs:54-58) maps `ErrorKind::WouldBlock` from `FlockArg::Lock{Exclusive,Shared}Nonblock` to a stable predicate Layer 4 can branch on. Layer 4 §2 (docs/layer4-implementation-addendum-v0.2.md:62-82) requires ingestion via `FileTapeFileJournal::open_shared_for_replay` + the consuming `load_committed()`, names `JournalError::is_lock_contended()` as the retry signal, requires Layer 4 to finish replay before opening its SQLite write transaction and never hold the journal flock across index updates, and specifies the inverse direction (a new 3c append session that observes contention because Layer 4 ingestion is briefly active returns a retryable open error or backs off within its session-open timeout). The conservative v0.7.2 FUA precondition (crates/remanence-parity/src/journal.rs:743-746) is now documented inline as a policy choice with a note that operators can request relaxation through an explicit allowlist; design §10.6 still owns the normative wording. Test coverage: file_journal_shared_reader_replays_without_truncating_torn_tail (crates/remanence-parity/src/journal.rs:1395) asserts the inode size is unchanged after reader replay over a deliberately torn tail, and file_journal_shared_reader_serializes_with_append_writer (crates/remanence-parity/src/journal.rs:1438-1487) exercises writer-blocks-reader, drop-then-reader-succeeds, multiple shared readers coexist, and writer-blocked-by-shared-readers — each path uses `assert!(err.is_lock_contended())`. Live verification per the codex entry shows the journal-wired QuadStor gate (quadstor_parity_journaled_session) plus the full ignored quadstor_parity suite passed 6/6 against /dev/sg0 with library 7CBAD9CF74 and bay 0x0100 after the operator loaded scratch RMN001L9 (cleanup: unloaded back to slot 1). Finding: no finding. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (700 passed / 0 failed / 8 ignored). Residual risk: I did not re-run the hardware-gated QuadStor suite myself (relying on the codex transcript and cleanup line); did not exercise concurrent shared-reader + active-writer lifecycle through a multi-process integration test; did not validate that `try_clone()` on Linux preserves the flock across forked-then-execed processes (only same-process descriptors were tested); did not line-by-line audit the Layer 4 v0.2 audit log or SQLite reconciliation against a prototype implementation.

author: claude
subject: plan: next work items after v0.7.2 journal hardening
summary: Forward plan recorded at operator request. Status snapshot: Layer 3c base (11.0–11.19) + v0.5 (11.5b/5c/8b/11b/13b/9c/19b) + v0.6 core (11.6b/6c/7b) landed through commit e825717 (Harden Layer 3c journal and promote v0.7.2 docs). Last review (uuid 71da4dd6-448d-4c56-a212-97c32bc3562c) found no finding; cargo test --workspace = 700 passed / 0 failed / 8 ignored. Remaining plan rows from docs/layer3c-design.md §13: 11.8c (checkpoint + BootstrapPlacementPolicy), 11.L5 (Layer 5 embedded SQLite catalog tailing committed bundles), 11.L5b (Layer 5 checkpoint-before-UNLOAD), 11.NoDB (no-database build gate), 11.18c (journal power-loss gate on akash ZFS), 11.19/11.19b (live MSL3040 + v0.5 hardware proof gates). Recommended ordering: (1) 11.8c checkpoint() + BootstrapPlacementPolicy first — pure code, unblocks Layer 5's clean-UNLOAD path, scoped per §6.1/§7.3 with test list assert-no-open-object / partial-epoch survives across resume / placement floor+taper+min-separation / mid-object checkpoint rejected. (2) 11.L5 SQLite catalog tail — Layer 4 v0.2 derived query index updated by tailing committed bundles, rebuild-catalog-from-journals command, wipe-and-rebuild equivalence test (§10.7). (3) 11.NoDB end-to-end gate — full write → unmount → remount → append → read → recover cycle with no DB process running, mock + scratch LTO (§14). (4) 11.18c akash power-loss gate — forced power cut → recovered committed prefix equals fsync-returned bundles, agrees with from-tape resume (§10.6, §14); test harness draftable now, run when hardware window opens. (5) Layer 5 API (spec-v0.2.md M6) deferred until session lifecycle + catalog tail close the end-to-end loop; the API is a translator over Layer 3 + Layer 4 (spec §3.2) and yields little verifiable progress without them. Layer 6 encryption (spec M7, SECURITY PROTOCOL IN/OUT + YubiKey KEK + Shamir) is cross-cutting and follows API per spec roadmap. This entry is planning only — no code changed, no commit, no push. No idref (not a review). Next codex slot should begin 11.8c.
